summary: This source provides a brief biographical overview of René Descartes, highlighting his foundational philosophical stance. It notes his rejection of past philosophical authority, his approach to writing on topics as if they were entirely new, and his famous declaration, 'I think, therefore I am'.

principles.rationale: The act of thinking is the fundamental proof of one's own existence.

principles.statement: Do not blindly accept the authority of previous philosophers.
principles.rationale: To establish original and foundational truths, one must approach subjects without being constrained by the views of past thinkers.
principles.source_quote: as if no one had written on these matters before.

signature_quotes.context: His best-known philosophical statement, originally 'cogito, ergo sum'.

signature_quotes.text: as if no one had written on these matters before.
signature_quotes.context: From the opening section of the Passions of the Soul, explaining his approach to the topic.

anti_patterns.description: Accepting the authority of previous philosophers.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It prevents a thinker from setting their views apart and establishing independent, foundational truths.
